lit.usageNote: In Black American English, often means excited, excellent, or intoxicated.
lit.culturalNote: Widely used in North American youth and Black vernacular.

fam.usageNote: Short for family; used to address close friends or crew.
fam.culturalNote: Common in Black English and hip-hop culture.

rizz.usageNote: Charisma, charm, or ability to attract others. Short for 'charisma.'
rizz.culturalNote: Exploded on TikTok; often 'rizz up' or 'pull rizz.'

sigma.usageNote: A lone-wolf type who succeeds without seeking approval; from 'sigma male' meme.
sigma.culturalNote: Part of alpha/beta/sigma internet masculinity slang.

gyatt.usageNote: Exclamation of surprise or approval; often used reactively.
gyatt.culturalNote: Spread via streaming and Gen Alpha platforms.

skibidi.usageNote: Nonsense word from viral Skibidi Toilet series; used for humor or randomness.
skibidi.culturalNote: Gen Alpha internet culture, YouTube Shorts.

fanum tax.usageNote: When someone takes a bite of your food; from streamer Fanum.

slay.usageNote: To do something exceptionally well; to look amazing.
slay.culturalNote: From ballroom and Black queer culture; mainstream Gen Z.

bussin.usageNote: Really good, especially food. 'This is bussin.'
bussin.culturalNote: Black American English, spread to Gen Z broadly.

no cap.usageNote: No lie; for real. 'No cap, that was fire.'

cap.usageNote: A lie or something false. 'That's cap.'
cap.culturalNote: Black American English; opposite of 'no cap.'

bet.usageNote: Okay, agreed, or 'I'm in.' Used as confirmation.
bet.culturalNote: Common in North American youth and Black English.

drip.culturalNote: Hip-hop and Black English; 'drip' = what you're wearing.

vibe.usageNote: The mood or feeling of a situation; to relax or get along. 'Good vibes.'
vibe.culturalNote: Widespread; roots in Black and hippie usage.

stan.usageNote: To be an obsessive fan (noun or verb). From Eminem's 'Stan.'
stan.culturalNote: Internet fandom; used across Gen Z and Millennial.

ghost.usageNote: To disappear from someone's life or ignore messages (ghosting).
ghost.culturalNote: Dating and social media; Millennial/Gen Z.

tea.usageNote: Gossip or juicy information. 'Spill the tea.'
tea.culturalNote: From Black queer and drag culture.

woke.usageNote: Aware of social injustice; sometimes used ironically.
woke.culturalNote: Black American English; broad political and ironic use.

period.usageNote: Emphasis: 'And that's that, period.' End of discussion.
period.culturalNote: Black English; used for finality or agreement.

fire.usageNote: Really good, hot, or excellent. 'That track is fire.'
fire.culturalNote: Hip-hop and Black English; mainstream Gen Z.

lowkey.usageNote: Kind of, a little bit; or kept quiet. 'Lowkey obsessed.'

highkey.usageNote: Obviously, very much; not hiding it. 'Highkey need this.'

yeet.usageNote: To throw something with force; or an exclamation of excitement.
yeet.culturalNote: Viral Vine/origin meme; Gen Z staple.

simp.usageNote: Someone who goes overboard to please another person (often romantically).

sus.usageNote: Suspicious; from Among Us. 'That's kinda sus.'

based.usageNote: Authentic, not caring what others think; or agreeing strongly.
based.culturalNote: From Lil B; internet and Gen Z.

mid.usageNote: Mediocre, average, not great. 'That was mid.'
mid.culturalNote: Black English and gaming; Gen Z.

slaps.usageNote: Really good, especially music. 'This song slaps.'
slaps.culturalNote: Widespread Gen Z; music and food.

go off.usageNote: To do something really well or to rant. 'She went off.'

deadass.culturalNote: Black American English; New York influence.

bestie.usageNote: Best friend; used affectionately or ironically.
bestie.culturalNote: Social media and Gen Z friendship language.

salty.usageNote: Upset, bitter, or annoyed. 'Don't be salty.'
salty.culturalNote: Gaming and general slang; Millennial/Gen Z.

squad.culturalNote: Black English and social media; Gen Z/Millennial.

flex.usageNote: To show off money, status, or something. 'Flex on them.'

adulting.usageNote: Doing grown-up tasks (bills, chores). 'I'm adulting today.'

fomo.usageNote: Fear of missing out. Anxiety about not being where the action is.

yolo.usageNote: You only live once. Used to justify a risky or fun choice.

hangry.usageNote: Angry because you're hungry. Hungry + angry.

ship.usageNote: To support a romantic pairing (relationship). 'I ship them.'

basic.usageNote: Mainstream, unoriginal, or predictable. 'Basic white girl.'

cancel.usageNote: To reject or boycott someone (cancel culture).

whatever.usageNote: Dismissive; I don't care. Often drawn out: 'Whatev-er.'

chill.usageNote: Relax; or cool, laid-back. 'Let's chill.' 'He's chill.'
chill.culturalNote: Gen X and hip-hop; still used by Millennials and Gen Z.

buzzkill.usageNote: Someone or something that ruins the mood or fun.
buzzkill.culturalNote: Gen X and Boomer; party and drug culture origins.

poser.usageNote: Someone who pretends to be something they're not; fake fan.
poser.culturalNote: Punk and alternative Gen X culture.

sellout.usageNote: Someone who abandons principles for money or mainstream success.
sellout.culturalNote: Punk and indie Gen X ethos.

meh.culturalNote: Simpsons origin; Gen X and internet.

cool.usageNote: Good, approved, fashionable. The all-purpose positive.
cool.culturalNote: Jazz and Beat era; Boomer and lasting.

dig.usageNote: To like or understand. 'I dig it.'

groovy.usageNote: Cool, great, in the groove. 'That's groovy.'

far out.usageNote: Amazing, mind-blowing, or weird in a good way.

hip.usageNote: In the know, fashionable. 'Hip to the scene.'
hip.culturalNote: Jazz and Beat; Boomer era; root of 'hippie.'
